mission.title: linkerd: Grafana links not updated when leveraging external grafana
mission.description: Grafana links not updated when leveraging external grafana. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify linkerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your linkerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
### What is the issue?
Using the helm-chart I have specified following values:
```yaml
global:
  cniEnabled: true
  grafanaUrl: https://externalurl.com
enforcedHostRegexp: '^linkerd\.(int|qa|stagin|prod)*\.io$'
installNamespace: false
grafana:

steps.title: Apply the fix for Grafana links not updated when leveraging external grafana
steps.description:
Thats definitely a valid concern! I tried googling around, and could find any solution which allows grafana to allow both i.e proxied and normal. 

Should we instead provide an option, which makes the the dashboard redirect to the given `grafanaUrl` instead of proxy'ing? obviously, that imples that
```yaml
global:
  cniEnabled: true
  grafanaUrl: https://externalurl.com
enforcedHostRegexp: '^linkerd\.(int|qa|stagin|prod)*\.io$'
installNamespace: false
grafana:
  enabled: false
```

steps.title: Confirm Grafana links not updated when leveraging… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd -n linkerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: Thats definitely a valid concern! I tried googling around, and could find any solution which allows grafana to allow both i.e proxied and normal.
codeSnippets:
global:
  cniEnabled: true
  grafanaUrl: https://externalurl.com
enforcedHostRegexp: '^linkerd\.(int|qa|stagin|prod)*\.io$'
installNamespace: false
grafana:
  enabled: false
Linkerd

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
